speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Brother of Gloucester, at Saint Albans field

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: This lady’s husband, Sir Richard Grey, was slain,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: His land then seized on by the conqueror.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Her suit is now to repossess those lands,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Which we in justice cannot well deny,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Because in quarrel of the house of York

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: The worthy gentleman did lose his life.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Your Highness shall do well to grant her suit;

speaker: RICHARD
text: It were dishonor to deny it her.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: It were no less, but yet I’ll make a pause.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Yea, is it so?

speaker: RICHARD
text: I see the lady hath a thing to grant

speaker: RICHARD
text: Before the King will grant her humble suit.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: He knows the game; how true he keeps the wind!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Silence!

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Widow, we will consider of your suit,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And come some other time to know our mind.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Right gracious lord, I cannot brook delay.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: May it please your Highness to resolve me now,

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: And what your pleasure is shall satisfy me.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, widow? Then I’ll warrant you all your lands,

speaker: RICHARD
text: An if what pleases him shall pleasure you.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Fight closer, or, good faith, you’ll catch a blow.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I fear her not, unless she chance to fall.

speaker: RICHARD
text: God forbid that, for he’ll take vantages.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: How many children hast thou, widow? Tell me.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: I think he means to beg a child of her.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Nay, then, whip me; he’ll rather give her two.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Three, my most gracious lord.

speaker: RICHARD
text: You shall have four if you’ll be ruled by him.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: ’Twere pity they should lose their father’s lands.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Be pitiful, dread lord, and grant it then.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Lords, give us leave. I’ll try this widow’s wit.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, good leave have you, for you will have leave

speaker: RICHARD
text: Till youth take leave and leave you to the crutch.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now tell me, madam, do you love your children?

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Ay, full as dearly as I love myself.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And would you not do much to do them good?

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: To do them good I would sustain some harm.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Then get your husband’s lands to do them good.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Therefore I came unto your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: I’ll tell you how these lands are to be got.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: So shall you bind me to your Highness’ service.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: What service wilt thou do me if I give them?

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: What you command that rests in me to do.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But you will take exceptions to my boon.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: No, gracious lord, except I cannot do it.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Ay, but thou canst do what I mean to ask.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Why, then, I will do what your Grace commands.

speaker: RICHARD
text: He plies her hard, and much rain wears the marble.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: As red as fire! Nay, then, her wax must melt.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Why stops my lord? Shall I not hear my task?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: An easy task; ’tis but to love a king.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: That’s soon performed because I am a subject.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Why, then, thy husband’s lands I freely give thee.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: I take my leave with many thousand thanks.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The match is made; she seals it with a cursy.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But stay thee; ’tis the fruits of love I mean.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: The fruits of love I mean, my loving liege.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Ay, but, I fear me, in another sense.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: What love, think’st thou, I sue so much to get?

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: My love till death, my humble thanks, my prayers,

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: That love which virtue begs and virtue grants.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: No, by my troth, I did not mean such love.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Why, then, you mean not as I thought you did.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: But now you partly may perceive my mind.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: My mind will never grant what I perceive

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Your Highness aims at, if I aim aright.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: To tell thee plain, I aim to lie with thee.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: To tell you plain, I had rather lie in prison.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Why, then, thou shalt not have thy husband’s lands.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Why, then, mine honesty shall be my dower,

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: For by that loss I will not purchase them.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Therein thou wrong’st thy children mightily.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Herein your Highness wrongs both them and me.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: But, mighty lord, this merry inclination

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Accords not with the sadness of my suit.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Please you dismiss me either with ay or no.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Ay, if thou wilt say “ay” to my request;

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: No, if thou dost say “no” to my demand.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: Then no, my lord; my suit is at an end.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The widow likes him not; she knits her brows.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: He is the bluntest wooer in Christendom.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Her looks doth argue her replete with modesty;

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Her words doth show her wit incomparable;

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: All her perfections challenge sovereignty.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: One way or other, she is for a king,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And she shall be my love or else my queen.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Say that King Edward take thee for his queen?

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: ’Tis better said than done, my gracious lord.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: I am a subject fit to jest withal,

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: But far unfit to be a sovereign.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Sweet widow, by my state I swear to thee

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: I speak no more than what my soul intends,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And that is, to enjoy thee for my love.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: And that is more than I will yield unto.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: I know I am too mean to be your queen

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: And yet too good to be your concubine.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: You cavil, widow; I did mean my queen.

speaker:
LADY
               
              GREY
text: ’Twill grieve your Grace my sons should call you father.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: No more than when my daughters call thee mother.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Thou art a widow and thou hast some children,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And, by God’s mother, I, being but a bachelor,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Have other some. Why, ’tis a happy thing

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: To be the father unto many sons.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Answer no more, for thou shalt be my queen.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The ghostly father now hath done his shrift.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: When he was made a shriver, ’twas for shift.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Brothers, you muse what chat we two have had.

speaker: RICHARD
text: The widow likes it not, for she looks very sad.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: You’d think it strange if I should marry her.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: To who, my lord?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Why, Clarence, to myself.

speaker: RICHARD
text: That would be ten days’ wonder at the least.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: That’s a day longer than a wonder lasts.

speaker: RICHARD
text: By so much is the wonder in extremes.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Well, jest on, brothers. I can tell you both

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Her suit is granted for her husband’s lands.

speaker: NOBLEMAN
text: My gracious lord, Henry, your foe, is taken

speaker: NOBLEMAN
text: And brought your prisoner to your palace gate.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: See that he be conveyed unto the Tower.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And go we, brothers, to the man that took him,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: To question of his apprehension.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Widow, go you along.—Lords, use her honorably.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Ay, Edward will use women honorably!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Would he were wasted—marrow, bones, and all—

speaker: RICHARD
text: That from his loins no hopeful branch may spring

speaker: RICHARD
text: To cross me from the golden time I look for.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And yet, between my soul’s desire and me,

speaker: RICHARD
text: The lustful Edward’s title burièd,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Is Clarence, Henry, and his son, young Edward,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And all the unlooked-for issue of their bodies

speaker: RICHARD
text: To take their rooms ere I can place myself.

speaker: RICHARD
text: A cold premeditation for my purpose.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, then, I do but dream on sovereignty

speaker: RICHARD
text: Like one that stands upon a promontory

speaker: RICHARD
text: And spies a far-off shore where he would tread,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Wishing his foot were equal with his eye,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And chides the sea that sunders him from thence,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Saying he’ll lade it dry to have his way.

speaker: RICHARD
text: So do I wish the crown, being so far off,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And so I chide the means that keeps me from it,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And so, I say, I’ll cut the causes off,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Flattering me with impossibilities.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My eye’s too quick, my heart o’erweens too much,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Unless my hand and strength could equal them.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Well, say there is no kingdom then for Richard,

speaker: RICHARD
text: What other pleasure can the world afford?

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll make my heaven in a lady’s lap

speaker: RICHARD
text: And deck my body in gay ornaments,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And ’witch sweet ladies with my words and looks.

speaker: RICHARD
text: O miserable thought, and more unlikely

speaker: RICHARD
text: Than to accomplish twenty golden crowns!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, Love forswore me in my mother’s womb,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And, for I should not deal in her soft laws,

speaker: RICHARD
text: She did corrupt frail Nature with some bribe

speaker: RICHARD
text: To shrink mine arm up like a withered shrub;

speaker: RICHARD
text: To make an envious mountain on my back,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Where sits Deformity to mock my body;

speaker: RICHARD
text: To shape my legs of an unequal size;

speaker: RICHARD
text: To disproportion me in every part,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Like to a chaos, or an unlicked bear-whelp,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That carries no impression like the dam.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And am I then a man to be beloved?

speaker: RICHARD
text: O monstrous fault to harbor such a thought!

speaker: RICHARD
text: Then, since this Earth affords no joy to me

speaker: RICHARD
text: But to command, to check, to o’erbear such

speaker: RICHARD
text: As are of better person than myself,

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll make my heaven to dream upon the crown,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And, whiles I live, t’ account this world but hell

speaker: RICHARD
text: Until my misshaped trunk that bears this head

speaker: RICHARD
text: Be round impalèd with a glorious crown.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And yet I know not how to get the crown,

speaker: RICHARD
text: For many lives stand between me and home;

speaker: RICHARD
text: And I, like one lost in a thorny wood,

speaker: RICHARD
text: That rents the thorns and is rent with the thorns,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Seeking a way and straying from the way,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Not knowing how to find the open air,

speaker: RICHARD
text: But toiling desperately to find it out,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Torment myself to catch the English crown.

speaker: RICHARD
text: And from that torment I will free myself

speaker: RICHARD
text: Or hew my way out with a bloody axe.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Why, I can smile, and murder whiles I smile,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And cry “Content” to that which grieves my heart,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And wet my cheeks with artificial tears,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And frame my face to all occasions.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll drown more sailors than the mermaid shall;

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll slay more gazers than the basilisk;

speaker: RICHARD
text: I’ll play the orator as well as Nestor,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Deceive more slyly than Ulysses could,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And, like a Sinon, take another Troy.

speaker: RICHARD
text: I can add colors to the chameleon,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Change shapes with Proteus for advantages,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And set the murderous Machiavel to school.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Can I do this and cannot get a crown?

speaker: RICHARD
text: Tut, were it farther off, I’ll pluck it down.
